title: Performance reporting for Immigration New Zealand :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Performance reporting for Immigration New Zealand
Immigration New Zealand (INZ) agrees specific performance goals with the government for each financial year. Find out how we are progressing against these goals.

## INZ performance measures 1 to 9
- This is a measure of work and visitor visas completed within 20 working days, for the last financial year and current financial year to date. 2025/2026 financial year Our target is 75% of work and visitor visas completed within 20 days. We have achieved a result of 86% for this financial year so far, from July 2025 to March 2026. Table 1: Percentage of work and visitor visas completed within 20 working days Date Percentage Commentary April 2026 84% We completed 84% of work and visitor visas on time in April. March 2026 81% We completed 81% of work and visitor visas on time in March. Visitor visas were completed faster than work visas. February 2026 82% We completed 82% of work and visitor visas on time in February. January 2026 81% We completed 81% of work and visitor visas on time in January. December 2025 84% We completed 84% of work and visitor visas on time in December. November 2025 89% We completed 89% of work and visitor visas on time in November. October 2025 89% We completed 89% of work and visitor visas on time in October. September 2025 91% We completed 91% of work and visitor visas on time in September. This is the best performance so far in this financial year. August 2025 90% We completed 90% of work and visitor visas on time in August. This is the best performance so far in this financial year. July 2025 89% We completed 89% of work and visitor visas on time in July. 2024/2025 financial year Our target was 75% of work and visitor visas completed within 20 days. We achieved a result of 82% for the financial year. Table 2: Percentage of work and visitor visas completed within 20 working days Date Percentage Commentary June 2025 87% We achieved our equal-best result this year in June, leading to a full-year result of 82% within time. This exceeds our target by 7%. May 2025 85% We completed 85% of work and visitor visas on time in May. Visitor scored 91% and work scored 71%. April 2025 87% We completed 87% of work and visitor visas on time in April. Visitor scored 93% and work scored 69%. March 2025 86% We completed 86% of work and visitor visas on time in March. Visitor scored 91% and work visas scored 74%. February 2025 80% We completed 80% of work and visitor visas on time in February. Visitor visas scored 83% and work visas scored 75%. This is the best result for work visas this year. January 2025 78% We completed 78% of work and visitor visas on time in January. Visitor visas scored 83% and work visas scored 66%. This is the best performance for work visas this financial year. December 2024 79% We completed 79% of work and visitor visas on time in December. Visitor visas scored 85% and work visas scored 65%. This is the best result for work visas this year. November 2024 72% We completed 72% of work and visitor visas on time in November. Visitor visas scored 78% and work visas scored 53%. October 2024 81% We completed 81% of work and visitor visas on time in October. Visitor visas scored 89% and work visas scored 61%. September 2024 84% We completed 84% of work and visitor visas on time in September. Visitor visas scored 92% and work visas scored 60%. Processing times for work visas remain affected by earlier policy changes. August 2024 82% We completed 82% of work and visitor visas on time in August. Visitor visas scored 91% and work visas scored 56%. Processing times for work visas have been affected by recent policy changes. July 2024 82% We completed 82% of work and visitor visas on time in July. Visitor visas scored 89% and work visas scored 58%. Processing times for work visas have been affected by recent policy changes.
This is a measure of work and visitor visas completed within 20 working days, for the last financial year and current financial year to date.

### 2025/2026 financial year
Our target is 75% of work and visitor visas completed within 20 days.
We have achieved a result of 86% for this financial year so far, from July 2025 to March 2026.
We completed 84% of work and visitor visas on time in April.
We completed 81% of work and visitor visas on time in March. Visitor visas were completed faster than work visas.
We completed 82% of work and visitor visas on time in February.
We completed 81% of work and visitor visas on time in January.
We completed 84% of work and visitor visas on time in December.
We completed 89% of work and visitor visas on time in November.
We completed 89% of work and visitor visas on time in October.
We completed 91% of work and visitor visas on time in September. This is the best performance so far in this financial year.
We completed 90% of work and visitor visas on time in August. This is the best performance so far in this financial year.
We completed 89% of work and visitor visas on time in July.

### 2024/2025 financial year
Our target was 75% of work and visitor visas completed within 20 days.
We achieved a result of 82% for the financial year.
We achieved our equal-best result this year in June, leading to a full-year result of 82% within time. This exceeds our target by 7%.
We completed 85% of work and visitor visas on time in May. Visitor scored 91% and work scored 71%.
We completed 87% of work and visitor visas on time in April. Visitor scored 93% and work scored 69%.
We completed 86% of work and visitor visas on time in March. Visitor scored 91% and work visas scored 74%.
We completed 80% of work and visitor visas on time in February. Visitor visas scored 83% and work visas scored 75%. This is the best result for work visas this year.
We completed 78% of work and visitor visas on time in January. Visitor visas scored 83% and work visas scored 66%. This is the best performance for work visas this financial year.
We completed 79% of work and visitor visas on time in December. Visitor visas scored 85% and work visas scored 65%. This is the best result for work visas this year.
We completed 72% of work and visitor visas on time in November. Visitor visas scored 78% and work visas scored 53%.
We completed 81% of work and visitor visas on time in October. Visitor visas scored 89% and work visas scored 61%.
We completed 84% of work and visitor visas on time in September. Visitor visas scored 92% and work visas scored 60%. Processing times for work visas remain affected by earlier policy changes.
We completed 82% of work and visitor visas on time in August. Visitor visas scored 91% and work visas scored 56%. Processing times for work visas have been affected by recent policy changes.
We completed 82% of work and visitor visas on time in July. Visitor visas scored 89% and work visas scored 58%. Processing times for work visas have been affected by recent policy changes.
- This is a measure of student visas completed within 30 working days, for the last financial year and current financial year to date. 2025/2026 financial year Our target is 75% of student visas completed within 30 working days. We have achieved a result of 83% for this financial year so far, from July 2025 to March 2026. Table 3: Percentage of student visas completed within 30 working days Date Percentage Commentary April 2026 69% We completed 69% of student visas on time in April. March 2026 73% We completed 73% of student visas on time in March. High levels of demand have affected timeliness. February 2026 74% We completed 74% of student visas on time in February. January 2026 80% We completed 80% of student visas on time in January. December 2025 86% We completed 86% of student visas on time in December. November 2025 87% We completed 87% of student visas on time in November. October 2025 87% We completed 87% of student visas on time in September. September 2025 86% We completed 86% of student visas on time in September. August 2025 89% We completed 89% of student visas on time in August. July 2025 88% We completed 88% of student visas on time in July. 2024/2025 financial year Our target was 75% of student visas completed within 30 working days. We achieved a result of 82% for the financial year. Table 4: Percentage of student visas completed within 30 working days Date Percentage Commentary June 2025 89% We achieved our best result of the year in June. This brings our year-to-date result to 82%, 7% better than target. May 2025 86% We completed 86% of student visas on time in May. This means four out of the past five months have been 85% or better. April 2025 83% We completed 83% of student visas on time in April. March 2025 86% We completed 86% of student visas on time in March. This is the equal best timeliness for Student since border reopening in 2022. February 2025 86% We completed 86% of student visas on time in February. This is the equal best timeliness for Student since border reopening in 2022. January 2025 85% We completed 85% of student visas on time in January. December 2024 86% We completed 86% of student visas on time in December. This is the best timeliness for Student since border reopening in 2022. November 2024 79% We completed 79% of student visas on time in November. October 2024 84% We completed 84% of student visas on time in October. This timeliness improvement was linked to increases in overall production. September 2024 72% We completed 72% of student visas on time in September. This is the first month-on-month drop for four months. August 2024 74% We completed 74% of student visas on time in August. This is the fourth consecutive monthly improvement in timeliness. July 2024 72% We completed 72% of student visas on time in July. This is the third consecutive monthly improvement in timeliness.
This is a measure of student visas completed within 30 working days, for the last financial year and current financial year to date.

### 2025/2026 financial year
Our target is 75% of student visas completed within 30 working days.
We have achieved a result of 83% for this financial year so far, from July 2025 to March 2026.
We completed 69% of student visas on time in April.
We completed 73% of student visas on time in March. High levels of demand have affected timeliness.
We completed 74% of student visas on time in February.
We completed 80% of student visas on time in January.
We completed 86% of student visas on time in December.
We completed 87% of student visas on time in November.
We completed 87% of student visas on time in September.
We completed 86% of student visas on time in September.
We completed 89% of student visas on time in August.
We completed 88% of student visas on time in July.

### 2024/2025 financial year
Our target was 75% of student visas completed within 30 working days.
We achieved a result of 82% for the financial year.
We achieved our best result of the year in June. This brings our year-to-date result to 82%, 7% better than target.
We completed 86% of student visas on time in May. This means four out of the past five months have been 85% or better.
We completed 83% of student visas on time in April.
We completed 86% of student visas on time in March. This is the equal best timeliness for Student since border reopening in 2022.
We completed 86% of student visas on time in February. This is the equal best timeliness for Student since border reopening in 2022.
We completed 85% of student visas on time in January.
We completed 86% of student visas on time in December. This is the best timeliness for Student since border reopening in 2022.
We completed 79% of student visas on time in November.
We completed 84% of student visas on time in October. This timeliness improvement was linked to increases in overall production.
We completed 72% of student visas on time in September. This is the first month-on-month drop for four months.
We completed 74% of student visas on time in August. This is the fourth consecutive monthly improvement in timeliness.
We completed 72% of student visas on time in July. This is the third consecutive monthly improvement in timeliness.
- This is a measure of people liable for deportation, who were deported or who deported voluntarily. Our target is 1,500 each year. We have achieved a result of 776 for this financial year so far, to February 2026. Table 5: Number of people liable for deportation, who were deported or deported voluntarily after compliance activity Financial year Total deportations Commentary 2025/2026 776 (as of February 2026) As of February 2026, a total of 776 deportations took place, of which 278 were enforced deportation types paid for by ICI. The remaining 498 were types carried out at the expense of the client. 2024/2025 1,316 A total of 1,316 deportations took place, of which 457 were enforced deportation types paid for by ICI. The remaining 859 were types carried out at the expense of the client. 2023/2024 916 A total of 916 deportations took place, of which 288 were enforced deportation types paid for by ICI. The remaining 628 were types carried out at the expense of the client.
This is a measure of people liable for deportation, who were deported or who deported voluntarily.
Our target is 1,500 each year.
We have achieved a result of 776 for this financial year so far, to February 2026.
776 (as of February 2026)
As of February 2026, a total of 776 deportations took place, of which 278 were enforced deportation types paid for by ICI. The remaining 498 were types carried out at the expense of the client.
A total of 1,316 deportations took place, of which 457 were enforced deportation types paid for by ICI. The remaining 859 were types carried out at the expense of the client.
A total of 916 deportations took place, of which 288 were enforced deportation types paid for by ICI. The remaining 628 were types carried out at the expense of the client.
- This is a measure of -mandated refugees and their immediate families who travelled to New Zealand, for the last financial year and current financial year to date. 2025/2026 financial year Our target is 1,500 UNHCR-mandated refugees and their immediate families who travelled to New Zealand. We have achieved a result of 1,073 arrivals for this financial year so far, from July 2025 to March 2026. Table 6: Number of UNHCR-mandated refugees and their immediate families who travelled to New Zealand Date Cumulative number Commentary April 2026 1275 There were 202 arrivals in April. March 2026 1073 There were 47 arrivals in March. Immigration NZ remains on track to achieve the full-year target. February 2026 1026 There were 160 arrivals in February. January 2026 866 There were 213 arrivals in January. December 2025 653 There were only 2 arrivals in December. November 2025 651 There were 204 arrivals in November, Afghanistan being the primary country with 62 arrivals, followed by Myanmar. October 2025 447 There were 46 arrivals in October. Myanmar was the primary source with 32 arrivals. September 2025 401 There were 196 arrivals in September. Afghanistan was the primary source with 60 arrivals, followed by Democratic Republic of Congo at 40 arrivals. August 2025 205 There were 85 arrivals in August. Syria was the primary source with 23 arrivals. July 2025 120 There were 120 arrivals in July. Myanmar was the primary source with 27 arrivals. 2024/2025 financial year Our target is 1,500 UNHCR-mandated refugees and their immediate families who travelled to New Zealand. We achieved a result of 1,507 arrivals for the financial year. Table 7: Number of UNHCR-mandated refugees and their immediate families who travelled to New Zealand Date Cumulative number Commentary June 2025 1,507 There were 174 arrivals in June. This brings the full-year number to 1,507, meaning we met our full-year target of 1,500. May 2025 1,333 There were 39 arrivals in May. Syria was the primary source with 14 arrivals. April 2025 1,294 There were 161 arrivals in April. Syria was the primary source with 32 arrivals. March 2025 1,133 There were 77 arrivals in March. Syria was the primary source with 51 arrivals. February 2025 1,056 There were 168 arrivals in February. There were 36 each from Eritrea and Myanmar, and 21 from Afghanistan. January 2025 888 There were 207 arrivals in January. 66 were from Syria, 30 from Myanmar, and 26 were from Democratic Republic of Congo. December 2024 681 There were just 7 arrivals in December, as it fell between major intakes. November 2024 674 There were 209 arrivals in November. 72 were from Syria, 43 from Myanmar, and 42 from Afghanistan. October 2024 465 There were 26 arrivals in October. 14 were from Iran. September 2024 439 There were 212 arrivals in September. 71 of these were from Syria, and 45 from Afghanistan August 2024 227 There were 170 arrivals in August. 47 of these were from Afghanistan and 37 from Syria. July 2024 57 There were 57 arrivals in July. 36 of these were from Syria with 14 from Pakistan.
This is a measure of -mandated refugees and their immediate families who travelled to New Zealand, for the last financial year and current financial year to date.

### 2025/2026 financial year
Our target is 1,500 UNHCR-mandated refugees and their immediate families who travelled to New Zealand.
We have achieved a result of 1,073 arrivals for this financial year so far, from July 2025 to March 2026.
There were 202 arrivals in April.
There were 47 arrivals in March. Immigration NZ remains on track to achieve the full-year target.
There were 160 arrivals in February.
There were 213 arrivals in January.
There were only 2 arrivals in December.
There were 204 arrivals in November, Afghanistan being the primary country with 62 arrivals, followed by Myanmar.
There were 46 arrivals in October. Myanmar was the primary source with 32 arrivals.
There were 196 arrivals in September. Afghanistan was the primary source with 60 arrivals, followed by Democratic Republic of Congo at 40 arrivals.
There were 85 arrivals in August. Syria was the primary source with 23 arrivals.
There were 120 arrivals in July. Myanmar was the primary source with 27 arrivals.

### 2024/2025 financial year
Our target is 1,500 UNHCR-mandated refugees and their immediate families who travelled to New Zealand.
We achieved a result of 1,507 arrivals for the financial year.
There were 174 arrivals in June. This brings the full-year number to 1,507, meaning we met our full-year target of 1,500.
There were 39 arrivals in May. Syria was the primary source with 14 arrivals.
There were 161 arrivals in April. Syria was the primary source with 32 arrivals.
There were 77 arrivals in March. Syria was the primary source with 51 arrivals.
There were 168 arrivals in February. There were 36 each from Eritrea and Myanmar, and 21 from Afghanistan.
There were 207 arrivals in January. 66 were from Syria, 30 from Myanmar, and 26 were from Democratic Republic of Congo.
There were just 7 arrivals in December, as it fell between major intakes.
There were 209 arrivals in November. 72 were from Syria, 43 from Myanmar, and 42 from Afghanistan.
There were 26 arrivals in October. 14 were from Iran.
There were 212 arrivals in September. 71 of these were from Syria, and 45 from Afghanistan
There were 170 arrivals in August. 47 of these were from Afghanistan and 37 from Syria.
There were 57 arrivals in July. 36 of these were from Syria with 14 from Pakistan.
- This is a measure of new registrations entered into the Live and Work NZ database with occupations that match those at skill levels 1 to 3. The measures are for the last financial year and current financial year to date. 2025/2026 financial year Our target is 50,000 registrations. We have achieved a result of 72,109 registrations for this financial year so far, to March. Table 8: Number of new registrations entered into the Live and Work NZ database with occupations that match those at ANZSCO skill levels 1 to 3 (target is 50,00 each year) Date Cumulative number Commentary April 2026 77,062 Another 5.0k registrations, dominated by India (0.7k) and then Nigeria (0.5k), with five nationalities around 0.3k each (Philippines, UK, USA, Pakistan, and UAE). March 2026 72,109 A quieter month with 5.4k registrations. After India with 0.7k registrations there were multiple countries around the 0.3-0.4k range (Philippines, UK, USA, and Pakistan). The YTD total is now at 60.4k, with three months remaining. February 2026 66,455 Another quieter month, but above last year. India (0.8k) was the largest source country, but there was surge in demand for Mexico to 0.5k. Demand from the USA more than halved to 0.3k, with the UK fairly stable at 0.3k. YTD 65k against a 50k target with 4 months. January 2026 60,915 A quieter month, but still above the seasonally adjusted target. There was a significant drop in Indian prospects, while United States numbers more than doubled to 0.7K, and increasing interest from Pakistan at 0.5K. December 2025 54,226 Another busy month with 11.3k skilled registrations, bringing the YTD number to 54.2k, above target, and almost precisely double the same month in the previous year. India remained the main source of interest (4.2k) with Philippines interest more than halving. November 2025 42,976 October 2025 27,700 We saw a surge in registrations due to changes to our website and increased marketing. Top nationalities were the Philippines, India, USA, and Morocco. ICT, Professional Services, Education & Training, Health & Medical and Engineering were the most common skilled registrations. September 2025 11,662 Registrations increased in September. Paid marketing campaign was conducted. ICT, Professional Services and Education and Training were the most common skilled registrations. We saw a noticeable increase from the Philippines and India. August 2025 1,881 Registrations held relatively stable. Farming & Primary, Hospitality, Retail & Sport and Professional Services were the most common skilled registrations. July 2025 934 We saw a drop in registrations with ICT, Professional Services and Education & Training the most common skilled registrations. 2024/2025 financial year Our target was 50,000 registrations. We achieved a result of 54,832 registrations for the financial year. Table 9: Number of new registrations entered into the Live and Work NZ database with occupations that match those at ANZSCO skill levels 1 to 3 Date Cumulative number Commentary June 2025 54,832 We saw further registrations in June, further exceeding our target. May 2025 52,201 We have reached our full-year target. The Philippines was our most common source of interest this month. April 2025 48,459 The rate of registrations has ticked up despite the cessation of paid marketing. We have seen continued higher-than-usual interest from the United States alongside a surge from Sri Lanka. March 2025 45,113 The rate of registrations remained lower with the cessation of paid marketing. We saw continued high levels of interest from the United States. February 2025 42,477 The rate of registrations slowed with the cessation of paid marketing. We saw noticeable interest from the United States. January 2025 38,725 We are seeing steady high levels of skilled registrations, with noticeable interest in January from The Philippines and Indonesia. December 2024 27,115 We saw a high level of skilled registrations, with noticeable interest from new source countries such as Malaysia, Thailand, and Indonesia. November 2024 19,711 We saw a continued surge in registrations, with a noticeable spike in demand from the United States around the date of their presidential election. October 2024 8,484 We saw a surge in registrations due to changes to our website and increased marketing. The Philippines and India were strongly represented markets. September 2024 4,526 We saw continued low volumes of registrations. The Philippines, United Kingdom, and United States were the nationalities most frequently represented. August 2024 3,412 We saw continued low volumes of registrations. The Philippines and United Kingdom were out strongest source markets. July 2024 1,989 We saw a drop in registrations as we shifted to a different marketing approach. Software Engineers, Secondary School Teachers, and Primary School Teachers were the three most common skilled registrations.
This is a measure of new registrations entered into the Live and Work NZ database with occupations that match those at skill levels 1 to 3. The measures are for the last financial year and current financial year to date.

### 2025/2026 financial year
Our target is 50,000 registrations.
We have achieved a result of 72,109 registrations for this financial year so far, to March.
Another 5.0k registrations, dominated by India (0.7k) and then Nigeria (0.5k), with five nationalities around 0.3k each (Philippines, UK, USA, Pakistan, and UAE).
A quieter month with 5.4k registrations. After India with 0.7k registrations there were multiple countries around the 0.3-0.4k range (Philippines, UK, USA, and Pakistan). The YTD total is now at 60.4k, with three months remaining.
Another quieter month, but above last year. India (0.8k) was the largest source country, but there was surge in demand for Mexico to 0.5k. Demand from the USA more than halved to 0.3k, with the UK fairly stable at 0.3k. YTD 65k against a 50k target with 4 months.
A quieter month, but still above the seasonally adjusted target. There was a significant drop in Indian prospects, while United States numbers more than doubled to 0.7K, and increasing interest from Pakistan at 0.5K.
Another busy month with 11.3k skilled registrations, bringing the YTD number to 54.2k, above target, and almost precisely double the same month in the previous year. India remained the main source of interest (4.2k) with Philippines interest more than halving.
We saw a surge in registrations due to changes to our website and increased marketing. Top nationalities were the Philippines, India, USA, and Morocco. ICT, Professional Services, Education & Training, Health & Medical and Engineering were the most common skilled registrations.
Registrations increased in September. Paid marketing campaign was conducted. ICT, Professional Services and Education and Training were the most common skilled registrations. We saw a noticeable increase from the Philippines and India.
Registrations held relatively stable. Farming & Primary, Hospitality, Retail & Sport and Professional Services were the most common skilled registrations.
We saw a drop in registrations with ICT, Professional Services and Education & Training the most common skilled registrations.

### 2024/2025 financial year
Our target was 50,000 registrations.
We achieved a result of 54,832 registrations for the financial year.
We saw further registrations in June, further exceeding our target.
We have reached our full-year target. The Philippines was our most common source of interest this month.
The rate of registrations has ticked up despite the cessation of paid marketing. We have seen continued higher-than-usual interest from the United States alongside a surge from Sri Lanka.
The rate of registrations remained lower with the cessation of paid marketing. We saw continued high levels of interest from the United States.
The rate of registrations slowed with the cessation of paid marketing. We saw noticeable interest from the United States.
We are seeing steady high levels of skilled registrations, with noticeable interest in January from The Philippines and Indonesia.
We saw a high level of skilled registrations, with noticeable interest from new source countries such as Malaysia, Thailand, and Indonesia.
We saw a continued surge in registrations, with a noticeable spike in demand from the United States around the date of their presidential election.
We saw a surge in registrations due to changes to our website and increased marketing. The Philippines and India were strongly represented markets.
We saw continued low volumes of registrations. The Philippines, United Kingdom, and United States were the nationalities most frequently represented.
We saw continued low volumes of registrations. The Philippines and United Kingdom were out strongest source markets.
We saw a drop in registrations as we shifted to a different marketing approach. Software Engineers, Secondary School Teachers, and Primary School Teachers were the three most common skilled registrations.
- This is a measure of visa applicants satisfied with the overall experience of applying for a visa, for the last financial year and current financial year to date. 2025/2026 financial year Our target is 80% of visa applicants satisfied with the overall experience of applying for a visa. We have achieved a result of 83% for this financial year so far, to quarter 3. Table 10: Percentage of visa applicants satisfied with the overall experience of applying for a visa Quarter Percentage Commentary 2025/26 Quarter 3 81% In quarter 3, work and resident plus visas (including PRV) recorded the highest satisfaction at 88%, followed by visitor (78%) and student visas (77%). 2025/26 Quarter 2 83% Satisfaction was 3% better than target. Our student and resident plus (including PRV) customers were the most satisfied, followed by work visa customers, then visitor visa customers. 2025/26 Quarter 1 85% Satisfaction was 5% better than target. Our work and Resident Plus customers were the most satisfied, followed by visitor visa customers, and then student visa customers. 2024/2025 financial year Our target was 80% of visa applicants satisfied with the overall experience of applying for a visa. We achieved a result of 81% for the financial year. Table 11: Percentage of visa applicants satisfied with the overall experience of applying for a visa Quarter Percentage Commentary 2024/25 Quarter 4 83% Our final quarter was our best this year, bringing full-year satisfaction to 81%, 1% higher than target. 2024/25 Quarter 3 80% We have re-weighted our customer survey results. This is to better represent the actual proportions of different customers. 2024/25 Quarter 2 81% Satisfaction was right on target. We achieved the best results for our Resident Plus and work visa customers. 2024/25 Quarter 1 82% Satisfaction was 1% better than target. Our work and Resident Plus customers were the most satisfied, followed by visitor visa customers, and then student visa customers.
This is a measure of visa applicants satisfied with the overall experience of applying for a visa, for the last financial year and current financial year to date.

### 2025/2026 financial year
Our target is 80% of visa applicants satisfied with the overall experience of applying for a visa.
We have achieved a result of 83% for this financial year so far, to quarter 3.
In quarter 3, work and resident plus visas (including PRV) recorded the highest satisfaction at 88%, followed by visitor (78%) and student visas (77%).
Satisfaction was 3% better than target. Our student and resident plus (including PRV) customers were the most satisfied, followed by work visa customers, then visitor visa customers.
Satisfaction was 5% better than target. Our work and Resident Plus customers were the most satisfied, followed by visitor visa customers, and then student visa customers.

### 2024/2025 financial year
Our target was 80% of visa applicants satisfied with the overall experience of applying for a visa.
We achieved a result of 81% for the financial year.
Our final quarter was our best this year, bringing full-year satisfaction to 81%, 1% higher than target.
We have re-weighted our customer survey results. This is to better represent the actual proportions of different customers.
Satisfaction was right on target. We achieved the best results for our Resident Plus and work visa customers.
Satisfaction was 1% better than target. Our work and Resident Plus customers were the most satisfied, followed by visitor visa customers, and then student visa customers.
- This is a measure of residence visa decisions rated as accurate, for the last financial year and current financial year to date. 2025/2026 financial year Our target is 85% of residence visa decisions rated as accurate. We have achieved a result of 93% for this financial year so far, to quarter 2. Table 12: Percentage of residence visa decisions rated as accurate Quarter Percentage Commentary 2025/2026 Quarter 2 95% We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 10% in the last quarter. 2025/2026 Quarter 1 91% We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 6% in the third quarter. 2024/2025 financial year Our target was 85% of residence visa decisions rated as accurate. We achieved a result of 92% for the financial year. Table 13: Percentage of residence visa decisions rated as accurate Quarter Percentage Commentary 2024/2025 Quarter 4 91% We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 6% in the final quarter. 2024/2025 Quarter 3 94% We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 9% in the third quarter. 2024/2025 Quarter 2 91% We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 6% in the second quarter. 2024/2025 Quarter 1 94% We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 9% in the first quarter.
This is a measure of residence visa decisions rated as accurate, for the last financial year and current financial year to date.

### 2025/2026 financial year
Our target is 85% of residence visa decisions rated as accurate.
We have achieved a result of 93% for this financial year so far, to quarter 2.
We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 10% in the last quarter.
We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 6% in the third quarter.

### 2024/2025 financial year
Our target was 85% of residence visa decisions rated as accurate.
We achieved a result of 92% for the financial year.
We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 6% in the final quarter.
We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 9% in the third quarter.
We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 6% in the second quarter.
We exceeded our quality target for residence visas by 9% in the first quarter.
- This is a measure of temporary visa decisions rated as accurate, for the last financial year and current financial year to date. 2025/2026 financial year Our target is 85% of temporary visa decisions rated as accurate.
